sources.name: Victorian Planning Authority Open Space
sources.limitation: Official metadata states the layer is not currently maintained; local-park coverage may undercount post-2019 growth-area additions.

sources.name: ABS ASGS Edition 3 Suburbs and Localities
ABS Census Mesh Block Counts
sources.role: Usual-resident population weights and residential category

sources.name: Victorian Parks and Conservation Reserves (PARKRES)
sources.role: Current supplement for major-park and nature-corridor context
sources.note: Supplement used only for major-park and nature-corridor layers, never for local-access scoring, because public pedestrian access is not encoded consistently enough for local-park eligibility. The ten-record inner-core onboarding batch used a fresh bounded PARKRES pull on 2026-07-19, the 24-record north-west/extended batch used another on 2026-07-21, and the 20-record Wednesday batch plus the subsequent five-record batch used another on 2026-07-22 (422 features each); PARKRES is a live dataset, while the four primary sources remained hash-identical to this manifest on all three dates.

includedAsLocalOpenSpace: VPA categories Parks and gardens, Sportsfields and organised recreation, Recreation corridor, Natural and semi-natural open space, and Conservation reserves when OS_STATUS is Existing/blank, OS_ACCESS is Open, OS_TYPE is Public open space, feature area is at least 0.05 ha, and the subcategory is not Golf course, Race course, Median park, Green buffer, or Outdoor shopping centre forecourts.
Water-body polygons without a coastal flag are excluded from the local-access layer rather than counted as usable parkland.
includedOnlyAsMajorOrNature: Filtered PARKRES reserve types matching Metropolitan park, Regional park, State park, National park, Coastal reserve, Reservoir park, Historic reserve, Other park, Nature conservation reserve, or Natural features reserve.
PARKRES supplement is merged only into major-park and nature-corridor proximity, not local-open-space coverage.
excluded: Private or restricted VPA land, including private golf courses, school grounds, cemeteries, tertiary campuses, services/utilities reserves, public-housing reserves, and transport reservations.
Median strips, green buffers, racecourses, golf courses, marine sanctuaries, port and coastal facilities, proposed reserves, and education areas in PARKRES.
ambiguousManualReview: VPA Services and utilities reserves or Transport reservations that also carry parks/natural/link subcategories were kept out and treated as QA review items rather than assumed to be usable greenspace.

Open public Conservation reserves or Natural and semi-natural polygons that are primarily water surfaces remain conservative: water-only polygons without a coastal flag are excluded from local access and only influence nature access where the surrounding reserve geometry remains eligible.
